description: Execute a plan until it is complete

steps.label: Planner Select And Store Next Plan

content: Use fresh disk reads and current git state, not conversational memory. Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` from disk first and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. Re-open the exact relative `plan_path` from disk even if you have read it before. Verify that the selected plan file exists in the current repository and that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. Re-check the current repository branch directly from git, for example with `git branch --show-current`, and re-check the most recent commit directly from git, for example with `git log --oneline -1`. If either of those scope checks fails, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Then verify that every additional repository still exists, is readable, and is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename. Re-check each additional repository branch directly from git, for example with `git -C <repo_root> branch --show-current`, and re-check its most recent commit directly from git, for example with `git -C <repo_root> log --oneline -1`. If an additional repository path is missing, invalid, or unreadable, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. If an additional repository no longer has a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename, stop and say repository branch scope has drifted and must be repaired before continuing. You MUST double-check to see if tasks have been added to the plan by another agent or if anything has changed. You must ensure you fully understand the plan and its remaining tasks before proceeding. You MUST read the whole plan again and CANNOT assume you understand it from before. Once you have done this, give me a summary of the remaining work.

steps.label: Task Execution And Blocker Repair Loop
Exit if no implementation task is available
steps.question: Answer from the selector result you just produced in the immediately preceding step. Answer yes only if you just wrote a state that does NOT resolve a current task for implementation, for example because plan repair is needed or the story is already complete. Answer no only if you just resolved one current task for this loop pass. Reply only as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Check for blocker before automated proof
steps.question: Use the same bound task from the immediately preceding deep repair step. If that step did not clearly resolve one bound task, answer yes. Otherwise run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py" --task-number <that-same-bound-task-number>`. Answer from that JSON output only. Answer yes iff `selected_task` is missing or `selected_task.live_blockers` is non-empty. Answer no only if `selected_task` exists and `selected_task.live_blockers` is empty. The parser already ignores inline `**BLOCKER**` references, `**BLOCKING ANSWER**`, and `**RESOLVED ISSUE**`. Reply only as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Run automated proof and fix issues
Check manual testing eligibility
steps.question: Use the same bound task from the immediately preceding automated-proof audit step. If that step did not clearly resolve one bound task, answer yes. Otherwise run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py" --task-number <that-same-bound-task-number>`. Answer yes iff any of the following are true: `selected_task` is missing; `selected_task.has_unchecked_subtasks` is true; `selected_task.has_unchecked_testing` is true; or `selected_task.live_blockers` is non-empty. Answer no only when the selected task exists, has no unchecked subtasks, has no unchecked testing, and has no live blockers, because only then should the task continue to the manual-testing phase whether its status is `__in_progress__` or `__done__`. Reply only as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Check for blocker before manual testing
steps.question: Use the same bound task from the immediately preceding automated-proof audit step. If that step did not clearly resolve one bound task, answer no. Otherwise run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py" --task-number <that-same-bound-task-number>`. Answer from that JSON output only. Answer yes iff `selected_task` exists and `selected_task.live_blockers` is non-empty. The parser already ignores inline `**BLOCKER**` references, `**BLOCKING ANSWER**`, and `**RESOLVED ISSUE**`. Reply only as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Check for blocker after manual testing
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-task.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-task.json`, and determine the bound task from what it contains rather than depending on an exact JSON shape. If it does not clearly resolve a task, answer no. Otherwise run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py" --task-number <bound-task-number>`. Answer from that JSON output only. Answer yes iff `selected_task` exists and `selected_task.live_blockers` is non-empty. The parser already ignores inline `**BLOCKER**` references, `**BLOCKING ANSWER**`, and `**RESOLVED ISSUE**`. Reply only as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Check if manual testing added follow-up work
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-task.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-task.json`, and determine the bound task from what it contains rather than depending on an exact JSON shape. If it does not clearly resolve a task, answer no. Otherwise run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py" --task-number <bound-task-number>`. Answer yes only if that bound task now has unchecked subtasks or unchecked testing steps. Answer only yes or no as JSON: {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Refresh current plan handoff after normalization
Research blocker solution and prove it
Refresh current plan handoff after planner edits

content: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first. Verify that every repository in scope is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename or report drift if it does not. Then run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py" --include-tasks` and use that JSON output as the source of truth for task state. Before answering completion, list each task's Task Status from the parser output, explicitly state whether the final task is __done__ or not using `final_task_status`, cite the relative `plan_path`, and state whether repository branch scope still matches the selected story.

steps.label: Checkpoint Push Before Task-Batch Completion Check
Check for completion
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. Verify that the selected plan file exists in the current repository and that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. If either of those checks fails, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Then verify that every additional repository still exists, is readable, and is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename. If an additional repository path is missing, invalid, or unreadable, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. If an additional repository no longer has a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename, stop and say repository branch scope has drifted and must be repaired before continuing. Then check the last 3 git commits in the current repository and in every additional repository, and run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py"`. Answer yes only if the parser reports `all_tasks_done: true` and `story_complete: true` and every repository in scope is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename. You must give only a yes or no answer. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Exit Minor-Fix Path Unless Review Is Minor-Only
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json` from disk. Answer yes iff `needs_minor_fix_path` is not true. Answer no only when `needs_minor_fix_path` is true, meaning all unresolved findings are minor-batchable and safe to fix inline before task-up. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Exit Minor-Fix Path If No Minor Finding Was Fixed
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/minor-review-fix-result.json` from disk. Answer yes iff `status` is not `fixed`, meaning no minor finding was fixed, no bounded inline minor finding remains, or the minor-fix path is blocked and must stop. Answer no only if `status` is `fixed`, meaning exactly one minor finding was fixed and can now be documented. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Exit Minor-Fix Path When No More Minor Findings Remain
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json` from disk after the documentation step. Answer yes iff `needs_minor_fix_path` is not true, meaning there are no more unresolved minor-only findings to fix in this review pass. Answer no only if `needs_minor_fix_path` is true, meaning another unresolved minor-only finding remains and should be fixed before the review is rerun. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Exit Task-Up Path Unless Unresolved Findings Need Tasks
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json` from disk. Answer yes iff `needs_task_up_path` is not true. Treat `resolved_minor_findings` as non-actionable for task-up. Answer no only when `needs_task_up_path` is true, meaning unresolved task-required findings or incomplete-review blockers must be encoded into the plan. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Ensure Unresolved Review Findings Became Tasks
Refresh Current Plan Handoff After Review Task-Up
Ensure Review-Created Testing Steps Are Correct
Create Or Update Simple Story After Review Task-Up
Exit Task-Up Path After Encoding Review Findings
steps.question: Answer yes after the task-up path has either encoded unresolved review findings into the plan or recorded why that path cannot continue. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"}.

steps.label: Generate Or Update Final Revalidation Task For Minor Fixes
Exit Review Loop If Review-Created Tasks Were Added
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json` from disk and re-open the current plan from `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json`. Answer yes iff `review_created_tasks_added_or_updated` is true or the current review loop added or updated a numbered plan task that must be executed before the story can close, including either task-required review-fix tasks or the single final revalidation task for minor review fixes. Answer no if no numbered task was added or updated and the review loop may either repeat or finish cleanly. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Repeat Review Loop If Minor Fixes Were Applied
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json` from disk. Answer no iff `needs_review_rerun_before_close` is true and `review_created_tasks_added_or_updated` is false, because the review must run again against the new HEAD. Answer yes iff `needs_review_rerun_before_close` is false and no numbered task was added, so the review loop can finish cleanly. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Checkpoint Push Before Story Completion Check
Check for completion
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. Verify that the selected plan file exists in the current repository and that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. If either of those checks fails, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Then verify that every additional repository still exists, is readable, and is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename. If an additional repository path is missing, invalid, or unreadable, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. If an additional repository no longer has a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename, stop and say repository branch scope has drifted and must be repaired before continuing. Then check the last 3 git commits in the current repository and in every additional repository, and run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py"`. Answer yes only if the parser reports `all_tasks_done: true` and `story_complete: true`. You must give only a yes or no answer. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.
